text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY — POST-OPERATIVE FOLLOW-UP NOTE

PATIENT: Denise Okafor
ATTENDING: Medical Oncology
VISIT TYPE: Post-operative oncology assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Routine post-operative oncology follow-up, approximately 4 weeks following bilateral mastectomy with tissue expander reconstruction. Review of final surgical pathology and discussion of adjuvant treatment planning.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old woman with West African descent and a strong hereditary cancer family history (mother with ovarian cancer at 52, maternal aunt with breast cancer at 44) who was diagnosed with Stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, upper outer quadrant, Nottingham grade 3. Ki-67 was 85%, TILs 35%, PD-L1 CPS 18 (22C3 assay). Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT) was identified on tumor-normal NGS and confirmed in peripheral blood. HRD score was positive at 68.

She completed neoadjuvant therapy per KEYNOTE-522 protocol: pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W with dose-dense AC (doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide Q2W x4 cycles with pegfilgrastim support), followed by weekly paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 x12 weeks with pembrolizumab. Her neoadjuvant course was complicated by two notable immune-related and chemotherapy-related toxicities:

1. Grade 2 immune-mediated thyroiditis during pembrolizumab Cycle 2 (thyrotoxic phase followed by hypothyroidism), managed with propranolol and ultimately levothyroxine 75 mcg daily. Pembrolizumab was held for one cycle and subsequently resumed. Thyroid function is currently stable on replacement therapy.

2. Grade 2 sensory peripheral neuropathy (bilateral hands and feet, stocking-glove distribution) emerging at week 7 of the paclitaxel phase, requiring paclitaxel dose reduction to 64 mg/m2 (80% dose). Duloxetine 30 mg daily was initiated. All 12 planned paclitaxel doses were completed. Neuropathy improved to grade 1 by end of treatment.

Post-neoadjuvant breast MRI demonstrated partial radiographic response: residual enhancing mass of 1.6 x 1.4 cm (down from 3.2 x 2.8 cm at diagnosis) and persistent left axillary node with cortical thickening at 6 mm. Given BRCA2 carrier status, partial response, and residual axillary nodal disease, the patient was extensively counseled regarding surgical options and elected left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection (levels I-II) and right prophylactic mastectomy with bilateral immediate tissue expander reconstruction. She underwent surgery without intraoperative complications approximately 4 weeks ago.

Final surgical pathology is now available and has been reviewed in detail with the patient today.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:

General: Fatigue moderate, improving steadily week over week since surgery. Denies fevers or chills.
Breast/wound: No wound infections, no erythema at incision sites. Drains removed at post-operative week 2 without difficulty.
Cardiovascular: No palpitations (prior thyrotoxic symptoms fully resolved).
Neurologic: Persistent mild numbness and tingling in bilateral feet and fingertips consistent with residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, not worsening. Functional — able to type and walk normally.
Endocrine: No symptoms of hypothyroidism. Taking levothyroxine consistently.
Gastrointestinal: Good appetite. No nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
Musculoskeletal: Left shoulder ROM mildly limited; patient has initiated physical therapy for post-axillary dissection exercises.
Psychiatric: Anxious about upcoming pathology discussion but emotionally supported by family. Engaged and asking detailed questions.

---

MEDICATIONS:
1. Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (autoimmune thyroiditis secondary to pembrolizumab)
2. Duloxetine 30 mg PO daily (chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy)
3. Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea
4. Ibuprofen 400 mg PO PRN pain (short course, post-operative)

ALLERGIES: NKDA

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Stage IIB TNBC, left breast (as above)
- Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (enrolled in BRCA2 registry)
- Immune-mediated thyroiditis (pembrolizumab-induced), now hypothyroid on replacement
- Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy, grade 1 (residual)
- No prior cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal disease

SOCIAL HISTORY:
High school biology teacher. Non-smoker, no alcohol. BMI 27.4 at presentation; current weight slightly lower given treatment-related weight fluctuations. Lives with husband and two children. Strong family and support structure. On leave from work during treatment but plans to return to teaching after adjuvant therapy initiation.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Mother: ovarian cancer, age 52. Maternal aunt: breast cancer, age 44. Genetic counseling referral placed previously; patient enrolled in BRCA2 registry. Family members undergoing cascade testing.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vitals: BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 72 bpm (regular), RR 16, Temp 98.5°F, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 71.2 kg.
ECOG Performance Status: 0.

General: Well-appearing woman in no acute distress, alert and conversational.
Head/Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Neck supple. Thyroid not palpably enlarged.
Chest: Bilateral mastectomy incisions well-healed, no erythema, dehiscence, or signs of infection. Bilateral tissue expanders palpable and appropriately placed. Initial tissue expander fills performed by plastic surgery. Left axillary surgical bed without palpable masses or hematoma. No palpable left axillary lymph nodes.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. Bilateral upper extremities symmetric; no clinical lymphedema of left arm at this time. Capillary refill brisk.
Neurologic: Grossly intact. Decreased monofilament sensation in bilateral feet (stocking distribution), consistent with residual grade 1 neuropathy. Finger dexterity intact.
Skin: No rash, no palmar erythema.

---

LABORATORY / DIAGNOSTIC RESULTS (obtained within past 1 week):

CBC: WBC 5.8 x10^9/L (differential: Neut 68%, Lymph 22%), Hgb 11.2 g/dL (slight post-operative decrease from prior nadir, trending toward recovery), Plt 234 x10^9/L.
Comprehensive metabolic panel: Na 139, K 4.1, Cr 0.78, BUN 14. ALT 26 U/L, AST 22 U/L, Alk Phos 78 U/L, Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL. Albumin 3.9 g/dL.
TSH: 1.8 mIU/L (within normal range on current levothyroxine 75 mcg). Free T4: 1.2 ng/dL (normal).

---

IMAGING RESULTS REVIEWED:

Post-neoadjuvant breast MRI (reviewed at prior pre-operative visit): Partial radiographic response. Residual 1.6 x 1.4 cm enhancing mass left UOQ with persistent axillary node cortical thickening at 6 mm. Discussed at prior visit. Informed surgical planning.

---

PATHOLOGY REVIEW — SURGICAL SPECIMENS:

Left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection (levels I-II): Residual invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3, measuring 1.8 cm in greatest dimension (ypT1c). All margins negative; closest margin 0.8 cm (deep margin). Residual high-grade DCIS present spanning 2.5 cm. Focal lymphovascular invasion present. Tumor bed fibrosis approximately 40% — reflecting partial treatment effect. Axillary lymph nodes: 3 of 16 positive for metastatic carcinoma (ypN1a); largest deposit 5 mm; no extranodal extension detected.

Pathologic staging: ypT1cN1a — non-pCR.
Miller-Payne grade: 3 (marked reduction in tumor cellularity, estimated 30-90% reduction).
Residual Cancer Burden (RCB): Index 2.8, RCB Class III — indicating extensive residual disease, which is the highest-risk category and carries the greatest risk of early distant recurrence.

Right prophylactic mastectomy: Benign fibrocystic changes only. No carcinoma identified.

These results were reviewed in detail with Ms. Okafor and her husband. She understood the implications of non-pCR and RCB Class III disease and engaged thoughtfully with questions about risk and next steps.

---

ASSESSMENT:

1. Stage IIB TNBC, left breast — status post neoadjuvant pembrolizumab + ddAC followed by paclitaxel + pembrolizumab, now post left modified radical mastectomy and right prophylactic mastectomy. Pathologic staging ypT1cN1a, RCB Class III (non-pCR) — high-risk residual disease.

2. Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant — already enrolled in BRCA2 registry; adjuvant PARP inhibitor therapy to be discussed (see below).

3. Hypothyroidism (pembrolizumab-induced) — stable on levothyroxine 75 mcg, TSH 1.8 mIU/L.

4. Residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy — stable, on duloxetine.

5. Post-surgical recovery — proceeding appropriately, bilateral tissue expanders in place, physical therapy underway for shoulder mobility.

---

PLAN:

1. ADJUVANT CAPECITABINE: Given RCB Class III residual disease after neoadjuvant therapy in TNBC, adjuvant capecitabine is indicated per CREATE-X trial data showing improved DFS and OS in patients with non-pCR TNBC. Plan: capecitabine 1000 mg/m2 PO BID days 1-14 of each 21-day cycle x 8 cycles (approximately 6 months total). BSA-based dosing will be calculated at first treatment visit; given BSA ~1.73 m2, anticipated dosing approximately 1500 mg BID. Labs (CBC, CMP) to be checked prior to each cycle. Patient counseled on hand-foot syndrome (most common toxicity — approximately 30% grade 2+), diarrhea, nausea, and mucositis. Will initiate urea-based moisturizer prophylactically. Plan to start approximately 5-6 weeks post-surgery once wound healing confirmed.

2. ADJUVANT PEMBROLIZUMAB CONTINUATION: Per KEYNOTE-522, total duration of pembrolizumab is 1 year (approximately 17 cycles including neoadjuvant). She has received 7 neoadjuvant cycles (Cycles 1, 3, 4 — with Cycle 2 held, plus Cycles 5, 6, 7 during paclitaxel phase). Remaining cycles planned in the adjuvant setting until approximately 17 total cycles are reached (~10 additional cycles). Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Q3W will resume concurrently with capecitabine. Labs (LFTs, TSH, CBC) to be checked prior to each pembrolizumab infusion. Patient has been counseled extensively on immune-related adverse events given prior thyroiditis. If significant new irAE occurs (particularly grade 3), pembrolizumab may need to be held or permanently discontinued.

3. POST-MASTECTOMY RADIATION THERAPY (PMRT): Radiation oncology was consulted in the pre-operative setting and has concurred with PMRT recommendation. Rationale: 3 positive lymph nodes at time of definitive surgery despite neoadjuvant chemotherapy meets standard criteria for post-mastectomy irradiation; regional nodal involvement and residual disease support locoregional benefit. Radiation oncology will irradiate the left chest wall and regional lymphatics (supraclavicular, axillary levels I-III, internal mammary chain). Planning CT simulation to be scheduled. Radiation to be coordinated with systemic therapy; typically initiated 6-8 weeks post-surgery. Detailed scheduling and field design deferred to radiation oncology.

4. ADJUVANT OLAPARIB — FUTURE CONSIDERATION: Per OlympiA trial data, adjuvant olaparib 300 mg BID x 1 year confers significant improvement in invasive disease-free survival for germline BRCA1/2 mutation carriers with high-risk HER2-negative breast cancer after standard neoadjuvant/adjuvant chemotherapy. Ms. Okafor clearly meets eligibility criteria given germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant and RCB Class III residual disease. However, olaparib cannot be safely administered concurrently with capecitabine and pembrolizumab due to overlapping toxicity (bone marrow suppression, GI toxicity) and limited safety data for the combination. Plan to reassess olaparib initiation after completion of capecitabine (~6 months from now) and once pembrolizumab is either completed or held. At that time, if the patient remains disease-free, initiate olaparib 300 mg BID x 52 weeks. This sequencing was discussed in detail with the patient, who is aware that olaparib is the next planned adjuvant step.

5. BREAST RECONSTRUCTION FOLLOW-UP: Plastic surgery team is managing tissue expander fills and planning future exchange to permanent implants after completion of radiation and systemic adjuvant therapy. Patient to continue close follow-up with plastic surgery team.

6. GENETIC COUNSELING / GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY: Patient remains in active follow-up with genetics team regarding BRCA2 carrier management and family member cascade testing. Gynecologic oncology referral for risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) discussion has been placed — timing to be coordinated with completion of adjuvant therapies. Patient advised that RRSO is the most effective risk-reduction strategy for BRCA2-associated ovarian cancer risk.

7. PHYSICAL THERAPY: Continue PT for left shoulder mobility. Early lymphedema surveillance with tape measure circumferential measurement of bilateral upper extremities at each visit.

8. MONITORING: CBC and CMP prior to each chemotherapy cycle and pembrolizumab dose. TSH every 6-8 weeks on levothyroxine. Follow-up appointment in 4 weeks for treatment initiation (capecitabine cycle 1 + pembrolizumab adjuvant cycle 1), pending healing assessment.

Time spent with patient: approximately 55 minutes. Questions answered. Patient verbalized understanding and agreement with treatment plan. Written educational materials provided regarding capecitabine, hand-foot syndrome precautions, and pembrolizumab immune-related toxicity monitoring.
